author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Ambrose Teaches that All Men Need God’s Help.
title: Ambrose Teaches that All Men Need God’s Help.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: In the same book the same St. Ambrose says again: “Now if Peter fell, who said, ‘Though all men shall be offended, yet will I never be offended,’ who else shall rightly presume concerning himself?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: David, indeed, because he had said, ‘In my prosperity I said, I shall never be moved,’ confesses how injurious his confidence had proved to himself: ‘Thou didst turn away Thy face,’ he says, ‘and I was troubled.’”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Pelagius ought to listen to the teaching of so eminent a man, and should follow his faith, since he has commended his teaching and faith.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Let him listen humbly; let him follow with fidelity; let him indulge no longer in obstinate presumption, lest he perish.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Why does Pelagius choose to be sunk in that sea whence Peter was rescued by the Rock?
